(pronounced [[ˈtêːləmɑrk]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Norwegian "Help:IPA/Norwegian") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Telemark.ogg "File:Telemark.ogg")) is a [county](/wiki/Counties_of_Norway "Counties of Norway") in [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). Telemark borders the counties of [Vestfold](/wiki/Vestfold "Vestfold"), [Buskerud](/wiki/Buskerud "Buskerud"), [Vestland](/wiki/Vestland "Vestland"), [Rogaland](/wiki/Rogaland "Rogaland") and [Agder](/wiki/Agder "Agder").[[4]](#cite_note-4) In 2020, Telemark merged with the county of [Vestfold](/wiki/Vestfold "Vestfold") to form the county of [Vestfold og Telemark](/wiki/Vestfold_og_Telemark "Vestfold og Telemark").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) On 1 January 2024, the county of Telemark was re-established after [Vestfold og Telemark](/wiki/Vestfold_og_Telemark "Vestfold og Telemark") was divided again.

**Telemark** (pronounced [[ˈtêːləmɑrk]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Norwegian "Help:IPA/Norwegian") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Telemark.ogg "File:Telemark.ogg")) is a [county](/wiki/Counties_of_Norway "Counties of Norway") in [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). Telemark borders the counties of [Vestfold](/wiki/Vestfold "Vestfold"), [Buskerud](/wiki/Buskerud "Buskerud"), [Vestland](/wiki/Vestland "Vestland"), [Rogaland](/wiki/Rogaland "Rogaland") and [Agder](/wiki/Agder "Agder").[[4]](#cite_note-4) In 2020, Telemark merged with the county of [Vestfold](/wiki/Vestfold "Vestfold") to form the county of [Vestfold og Telemark](/wiki/Vestfold_og_Telemark "Vestfold og Telemark").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) On 1 January 2024, the county of Telemark was re-established after [Vestfold og Telemark](/wiki/Vestfold_og_Telemark "Vestfold og Telemark") was divided again.

The name *Telemark* means the "[mark](/wiki/March_(territorial_entity) "March (territorial entity)") of the [Thelir](/wiki/Thelir "Thelir")", the ancient [North Germanic tribe](/wiki/North_Germanic_peoples "North Germanic peoples") that inhabited what is now known as [Upper Telemark](/wiki/Upper_Telemark "Upper Telemark") in the [Migration Period](/wiki/Migration_Period "Migration Period") and the [Viking Age](/wiki/Viking_Age "Viking Age").

In the Middle Ages, the agricultural society of Upper Telemark was considered the most violent region of Norway.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Today, half of the buildings from medieval times in Norway are located here.[[8]](#cite_note-8) The dialects spoken in Upper Telemark also retain more elements of [Old Norse](/wiki/Old_Norse "Old Norse") than those spoken elsewhere in the country.[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9) Upper Telemark is also known as the birthplace of [skiing](/wiki/Skiing "Skiing").[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9)

The southern part of Telemark, [Grenland](/wiki/Grenland "Grenland"), is more urban and influenced by trade with the [Low Countries](/wiki/Low_Countries "Low Countries"), [northern Germany](/wiki/Northern_Germany "Northern Germany"), [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark") and the [British Isles](/wiki/British_Isles "British Isles").

Telemark has been one of Norway's most important industrial regions for centuries, marked in particular by the [Norske Skog Union](/wiki/Norske_Skog_Union "Norske Skog Union") paper mills in [Grenland](/wiki/Grenland "Grenland") and the [Norsk Hydro](/wiki/Norsk_Hydro "Norsk Hydro") heavy water and fertilizer production in [Upper Telemark](/wiki/Upper_Telemark "Upper Telemark").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Telemark county was re-established on 1 January 2024, following a vote of the county council of Vestfold og Telemark on 15 February 2022 to split the newly established county into its respective counties that existed before the merger took place; Telemark and Vestfold.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Telemark&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0b/Eidsborg_stavkirke_i_Tokke.jpg/500px-Eidsborg_stavkirke_i_Tokke.jpg)](/wiki/File:Eidsborg_stavkirke_i_Tokke.jpg)

The 13th-century [Eidsborg Stave Church](/wiki/Eidsborg_Stave_Church "Eidsborg Stave Church") in [Tokke Municipality](/wiki/Tokke_Municipality "Tokke Municipality"), Upper Telemark

Telemark county was established as the fief Bratsberg in the late Middle Ages, during [Norway's union with Denmark](/wiki/Denmark%E2%80%93Norway "Denmark–Norway"). With the introduction of absolute monarchy in 1662 it became a county, and it was renamed Telemark in 1919 and was a county until 2020. The county administration was in the port town [Skien](/wiki/Skien_(town) "Skien (town)"), which was in the early modern period Norway's most important city, ahead of Christiania.

Telemark consists of several distinct historical regions. It takes its name from the largest of them, which is now called [Upper Telemark](/wiki/Upper_Telemark "Upper Telemark"), but which was historically simply called Telemark. Telemark is named for the [Thelir](/wiki/Thelir "Thelir") (Þilir in Old Norse), the ancient North Germanic tribe that inhabited what is now known as Upper Telemark since the [Migration Period](/wiki/Migration_Period "Migration Period") and during the [Viking Age](/wiki/Viking_Age "Viking Age"). The Norse form of the name was *Þelamǫrk*. The first element is the genitive plural case of *Þilir* while the last element is *mǫrk* "woodland, borderland, [march](/wiki/March_(territory) "March (territory)")".

Traditional Telemark, i.e. Upper Telemark, is located in the inland and comprises more than two thirds of it according to its traditional definition. Both in medieval times and later (Upper) Telemark was the region of Norway with the most self-owning farmers.[[11]](#cite_note-11) It retained [Norse](/wiki/Norsemen "Norsemen") culture to a larger degree than any other region in Norway, with respect to its more egalitarian organisation of society, religion, traditional values and language. Thus the people of Telemark were often described during the Middle Ages and early modern era as the most violent in Norway.[[12]](#cite_note-12) The dialects of Upper Telemark are also the dialects of Norwegian that are closest to [Old Norse](/wiki/Old_Norse "Old Norse"). The farmers of Telemark were marked by a strong-willed conservatism and belief in their traditional values that often defied the central authorities of Denmark-Norway; for example they held on to aspects of both [Old Norse religion](/wiki/Old_Norse_religion "Old Norse religion") and later of [Catholicism](/wiki/Catholicism "Catholicism") longer than other regions in Norway. (Upper) Telemark traditionally lacks cities entirely.

[Grenland](/wiki/Grenland "Grenland") and the Skien fjord are flatter regions located closer to or at the coast. Historically Grenland referred to what is now called [Midt-Telemark](/wiki/Midt-Telemark "Midt-Telemark"), but over time the name Grenland has come to refer to the Skien fjord area. The latter is traditionally characterized by its cities and its involvement in seafaring and trade. It also includes several larger agricultural properties and estates, as well as industry. The culture and social structure are more urban, far less traditional, more influenced by contact with continental Europe and far less egalitarian. The most important city of the region, [Skien](/wiki/Skien_(town) "Skien (town)"), was historically one of Norway's most important cities, although its importance declined after the Napoleonic Wars. The playwright [Henrik Ibsen](/wiki/Henrik_Ibsen "Henrik Ibsen") was a native of Skien, and many of his plays are set in places reminiscent of the city and area.

During the Dano-Norwegian union the traditional regions of Telemark and Grenland/the Skien fjord became the fief (*len*) and later county (*[amt](/wiki/Amt_(subnational_entity) "Amt (subnational entity)")*) of Bratsberg (Bradsberg). The fief and county was named after the farm Bratsberg, since this was the seat of the governor. In 1919 Bratsberg county was renamed Telemark. Despite this, Grenland retains a separate identity that is distinct from Telemark proper; the minority in the Storting voted for the name Grenland–Telemark in 1918.

Upper Telemark, particularly [Kviteseid Municipality](/wiki/Kviteseid_Municipality "Kviteseid Municipality"), is known as the birthplace of [skiing](/wiki/Skiing "Skiing") as a modern sport. Telemark lent its name to [Telemark skiing](/wiki/Telemark_skiing "Telemark skiing"), a style invented by [Sondre Norheim](/wiki/Sondre_Norheim "Sondre Norheim"), and the characteristic [Telemark landing](/wiki/Telemark_landing "Telemark landing") of [ski jumping](/wiki/Ski_jumping "Ski jumping"). Telemark is also known as the centre of the *[Bunad](/wiki/Bunad "Bunad")* movement. Telemark has more buildings from medieval times than any other Norwegian region.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Telemark&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/Summer_landscape_Telemark_%282690578882%29.jpg/250px-Summer_landscape_Telemark_%282690578882%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Summer_landscape_Telemark_(2690578882).jpg)

Mountain landscape in [Vinje Municipality](/wiki/Vinje_Municipality "Vinje Municipality"), [Upper Telemark](/wiki/Upper_Telemark "Upper Telemark")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/ba/Langesund_og_Lang%C3%B8ya_01.jpg/250px-Langesund_og_Lang%C3%B8ya_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Langesund_og_Lang%C3%B8ya_01.jpg)

Coastal landscape in [Langesund](/wiki/Langesund "Langesund"), [Lower Telemark](/wiki/Nedre_Telemark "Nedre Telemark")

Telemark is located in southeastern Norway, extending from the [Hardangervidda](/wiki/Hardangervidda "Hardangervidda") mountain plateau in the North to the [Skagerrak](/wiki/Skagerrak "Skagerrak") coast in the South. Telemark has a varied and scenic landscape, including a rugged coastline, valleys, lakes, hills mountains, and mountain plateaus.[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9)

Southern Telemark is the location of the Gea Norvegica [UNESCO Global Geopark](/wiki/UNESCO_Global_Geoparks "UNESCO Global Geoparks") (UGG), established in 2006.[[13]](#cite_note-13) The UGG carries significance to Norway with an abundance of [larvikite](/wiki/Larvikite "Larvikite") along the [Oslo Rift](/wiki/Oslo_Rift "Oslo Rift"), [Paleozoic](/wiki/Paleozoic "Paleozoic") fossils, geological artifacts of [glacial retreat](/wiki/Glacial_motion "Glacial motion") from the [last glacial maximum](/wiki/Last_Glacial_Maximum "Last Glacial Maximum"), and rocks from the [Sveconorwegian Orogeny](/wiki/Sveconorwegian_orogeny "Sveconorwegian orogeny"). It is the first UGG in Scandinavia.

## Infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Telemark&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Infrastructure")]

The international road [E18](/wiki/European_route_E18 "European route E18") goes through the southern parts of Telemark, namely [Grenland](/wiki/Grenland "Grenland") and the municipality of [Kragerø](/wiki/Krager%C3%B8 "Kragerø"). [E134](/wiki/European_route_E134 "European route E134"), another important motorway and the fastest route between [Oslo](/wiki/Oslo "Oslo") and [Bergen](/wiki/Bergen "Bergen"), goes through the municipalities of [Vinje](/wiki/Vinje_Municipality "Vinje Municipality"), [Tokke](/wiki/Tokke_Municipality "Tokke Municipality"), [Kviteseid](/wiki/Kviteseid_Municipality "Kviteseid Municipality"), [Seljord](/wiki/Seljord_Municipality "Seljord Municipality"), [Hjartdal](/wiki/Hjartdal_Municipality "Hjartdal Municipality"), and [Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality"). [RV36](/w/index.php?title=RV36&action=edit&redlink=1 "RV36 (page does not exist)"), stretching from [Porsgrunn](/wiki/Porsgrunn_Municipality "Porsgrunn Municipality") to Seljord, links the E18 and E134 motorways.

Telemark is well served by railways. The [Sørlandet Line](/wiki/S%C3%B8rlandet_Line "Sørlandet Line") runs through the traditional districts of Vestmar and Midt-Telemark, serving the municipalities of [Drangedal](/wiki/Drangedal_Municipality "Drangedal Municipality"), [Nome](/wiki/Nome_Municipality "Nome Municipality"), and [Midt-Telemark](/wiki/Midt-Telemark_Municipality "Midt-Telemark Municipality"). Grenland is primarily served by the [Vestfold Line](/wiki/Vestfold_Line "Vestfold Line"), but also has connections through the [Bratsberg Line](/wiki/Bratsberg_Line "Bratsberg Line") which runs between the towns of [Skien](/wiki/Skien_(town) "Skien (town)") and [Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)").

From Langesund, [Fjordline](/wiki/Fjord_Line "Fjord Line") operates ferry services to [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") and [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark").

The main bus lines in the region are operated by [Telemark Bilruter](/wiki/Telemark_Bilruter "Telemark Bilruter"), serving western and middle parts of the region, and [Nettbuss](/wiki/Nettbuss "Nettbuss") which serves the middle, eastern and southern parts of the region. [Drangedal Bilruter](/w/index.php?title=Drangedal_Bilruter&action=edit&redlink=1 "Drangedal Bilruter (page does not exist)") serves the [Vestmar](/wiki/Vestmar "Vestmar") region.

The [coat of arms of Telemark](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Telemark "Coat of arms of Telemark") is from modern times (1970). It shows an old type of [battle axe](/wiki/Battle_axe "Battle axe"), significant for the county.

The county coat of arms valid from 1 January 2024 is a redesigned version of the county coat of arms for Telemark county from 1970 until the county merger in 2020.

## Districts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Telemark&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Districts")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/%C3%98vre_Telemark_prosti.svg/250px-%C3%98vre_Telemark_prosti.svg.png)](/wiki/File:%C3%98vre_Telemark_prosti.svg)

Telemark county with [Upper Telemark](/wiki/Upper_Telemark "Upper Telemark") (traditional Telemark) in red

The county is conventionally divided into traditional districts. Traditionally the county is mainly divided into [Upper Telemark](/wiki/Upper_Telemark "Upper Telemark") (historically called simply Telemark or more recently Telemark proper) and [Grenland](/wiki/Grenland "Grenland"). Upper Telemark is sometimes subdivided into [Vest-Telemark](/wiki/Vest-Telemark "Vest-Telemark") and [Aust-Telemark](/wiki/Aust-Telemark "Aust-Telemark"). The name [Lower Telemark](/w/index.php?title=Lower_Telemark&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lower Telemark (page does not exist)") traditionally refers to Grenland and [Midt-Telemark](/wiki/Midt-Telemark "Midt-Telemark"), but was more of an administrative region than a cultural one. Regardless of definition, Upper Telemark constitutes the largest part by far. For example, the modern provostship of Upper Telemark comprises 12 municipalities and more than 80% of Telemark, also including Midt-Telemark.

An additional district, [Vestmar](/wiki/Vestmar "Vestmar") is disputed. The district borders of this county are highly overlapping and to a certain extent undefined and/or disputed.
